patch.summary: Lower min_support from 30 to 10 to allow candidate rules to survive with only 3 days of data
why: With only 3 trading days and 14759 snapshots, min_support=30 requires a rule to fire on ~30 qualifying windows — but with extreme day concentration risk at 3 days, many promising patterns cannot reach 30 hits while also passing day-concentration checks
6162 candidates were generated but 0 rules were promoted, strongly suggesting the support threshold is the binding constraint at this early research stage
The maturity tier is 'research' with watchlist-only output, so loosening support now is low-risk — rules still cannot promote to live until maturity gates are passed with more days
expected_benefit: Candidates should begin appearing on the watchlist, enabling the system to start tracking durability and overlap diagnostics across future nights
Credit assignment on future nights becomes possible because we will have watchlist rules to evaluate rather than an empty set
No live risk since maturity gating prevents premature promotion regardless of support threshold
risk: Lower support allows noisier patterns onto the watchlist, but with only 3 days this is unavoidable — the maturity gate is the real robustness filter, not min_support at this stage
patch.acceptance_hint: Accept if promoted watchlist candidates > 0 AND at least some candidates show non-trivial composite scores; revert if watchlist is flooded with >500 near-duplicate low-quality rules

patch.summary: Lower min_support from 30 to 15 to allow candidate rules to survive with only 3 days of data
why: With only 3 trading days and 14759 snapshots, min_support=30 requires a pattern to appear ~30 times across just 3 days, which is a high bar that likely explains why 6162 candidates were generated but 0 were promoted
The system is in research tier with zero promoted rules and zero experiments — the pipeline is generating candidates but the support threshold is filtering everything out before promotion

Lowering to 15 still requires meaningful recurrence (~5 per day average) but gives nascent patterns a chance to surface onto the watchlist where walk-forward can evaluate them as more data accumulates
expected_benefit: Rules should begin appearing on the watchlist/candidate list, enabling the walk-forward evaluator to begin scoring them
The system can start building a research pipeline of candidates that will either strengthen or wash out as days accumulate
No impact on live trading since maturity tier is research and maturity gating prevents premature promotion
risk: Lower support could allow noisier patterns through — mitigated by the fact that maturity gating, overlap checks, and walk-forward evaluation are still active downstream
patch.acceptance_hint: Accept if promoted candidate count rises above 0 and at least some candidates show positive composite scores with occurrence spread across multiple days rather than concentrated on one day

decision.patch_summary: first_run
decision.reason: First run produced no rules. No champion set.

patch.summary: Lower min_support from 30 to 10 to allow candidate rules to survive walk-forward with only 3 trading days of data
why: With only 3 trading days and 14759 snapshots, the current min_support=30 requires a rule to fire on ~30 qualifying windows — but walk-forward splits this tiny sample further, making it nearly impossible for any candidate to pass support checks in each fold
Previous experiment lowered to 15 but still produced 0 promoted rules from 6162 candidates, indicating 15 is still too high for the current data volume
6162 candidates were generated but 0 survived, meaning the bottleneck is the walk-forward support filter, not candidate generation quality
expected_benefit: Some candidates should now survive walk-forward evaluation, populating the watchlist and enabling meaningful diagnostics about rule quality, family performance, and overlap
This unblocks the entire research loop — without any promoted rules we cannot measure anything else or make informed future mutations
risk: With min_support=10 on 3 days, any surviving rules will be low-confidence and potentially overfit to recent regime — but maturity gating is at 'research/watchlist only' so no live risk exists; rules must still pass many more days before promotion
patch.acceptance_hint: Accept if at least 1 candidate rule survives to the watchlist with reasonable composite score, enabling downstream diagnostics; reject if still 0 rules or if candidates are clearly degenerate single-snapshot artifacts

patch.summary: Lower min_support from 30 to 10 to allow candidate rules to survive walk-forward with only 3 days of data
why: With only 3 trading days and 14,759 snapshots, min_support=30 requires a rule to fire on 30+ qualifying snapshots that pass walk-forward — the previous attempt at 15 also produced zero promoted rules, indicating the threshold is still too high relative to the tiny sample
6,162 candidates were generated but zero survived promotion, meaning the support filter is the binding constraint killing every candidate before it can reach the watchlist

At 3 days the system is in research tier where the goal is watchlist population, not live promotion — lowering to 10 lets us observe which rule families and feature families produce the most promising candidates for credit assignment as data accumulates
expected_benefit: Non-zero candidate rules should survive to the watchlist, enabling the first meaningful diagnostics on rule family performance, overlap, and day concentration
Overlap and fragility guardrails remain active so low-quality rules will still be filtered — we just widen the funnel enough to populate the watchlist
As days accumulate past 5-10, min_support can be raised back; this is a bootstrapping adjustment for the cold-start phase
risk: With support=10 on 3 days, some promoted watchlist candidates may be day-concentrated or regime-concentrated — but since maturity is research-only and no rules go live, the downside is limited to noisy early diagnostics
patch.acceptance_hint: Accept if at least 1 candidate rule reaches the watchlist with non-degenerate day distribution; reject if still zero rules or if all candidates fire on a single day only

patch.summary: Lower min_support from 30 to 10 to allow candidate rules to survive walk-forward with only 4 trading days of data
why: With only 4 trading days and 18949 snapshots, the current min_support=30 requires a rule to fire on ~30 qualifying snapshots across walk-forward folds, which is extremely difficult with so few days — 6162 candidates were generated but zero survived promotion

The previous experiment tried min_support=15 but was rejected because no champion set existed yet (first run); now we have a baseline of 0 promoted rules, so any surviving rules would be a strict improvement and the acceptance logic should work

Setting min_support=10 is more aggressive than the rejected 15 but is necessary given the tiny 4-day sample — the system needs to discover at least some candidate patterns to begin the research loop, and other guardrails (max_overlap, min_composite_score, maturity gating) still prevent fragile rules from reaching live
expected_benefit: At least some candidate rules should survive walk-forward filtering and appear on the watchlist, enabling the research loop to begin producing diagnostics about rule family quality, overlap, regime concentration, and skip effectiveness
Maturity gating (research tier) ensures nothing reaches live prematurely — this only unlocks watchlist-level candidates for further study
risk: Rules with support=10 on 4 days may be overfit to a narrow regime; however the research maturity tier prevents any live action, and as days accumulate the naturally rising support counts will filter out fragile patterns
patch.acceptance_hint: Patch is worth keeping if at least 1 candidate rule is promoted to the watchlist with composite_score > 0 and the rule fires on more than 1 distinct trading day

patch.summary: Lower min_support threshold from 30 to 10 to allow candidate rules to pass filtering given only 4 trading days of data
why: The system has only 4 trading days and 18949 snapshots but generated 6162 candidates with 0 promotions — min_support=30 is almost certainly killing every candidate because no rule can fire 30+ times across just 4 days with sufficient consistency

The previous experiment tried min_support=15 but was rejected because no champion set existed yet (first run); now with a baseline established, lowering to 10 gives candidates a realistic chance to accumulate support while still requiring meaningful repetition (~2-3 fires per day minimum)
With 18949 snapshots across 4 days (~4737/day), a min_support of 10 requires a rule to fire on roughly 0.2% of snapshots — still a meaningful statistical bar but achievable for state-detection rules
expected_benefit: Rules should begin surviving the support filter, allowing the walk-forward evaluator to actually score and rank candidates for the first time
The system can begin building a champion set and watchlist, enabling meaningful credit assignment on future mutations
Overlap, day-concentration, and regime-concentration diagnostics will finally have data to report on
risk: With only 4 days, even rules meeting support=10 may be fragile single-regime artifacts; however the walk-forward evaluator and maturity gating (research tier) already guard against premature promotion to live
patch.acceptance_hint: At least 1 candidate rule survives to the watchlist with non-trivial composite score, enabling the system to produce its first meaningful champion set for future differential testing

patch.summary: Lower min_support from 30 to 10 to allow candidate rules to survive walk-forward with only 4 trading days of data
why: With only 4 trading days and 18949 snapshots, min_support=30 requires a candidate rule to fire on 30+ qualifying instances across walk-forward folds, which is extremely hard to achieve with so little history — 6162 candidates were generated but zero survived promotion

The previous experiment tried min_support=15 but was rejected because no champion set existed yet (not because it was a bad idea); the system still has zero promoted rules and zero skip rules, so the pipeline is completely blocked

Lowering to 10 is more aggressive but still requires each rule to fire across multiple distinct snapshots, providing meaningful statistical support while unblocking the pipeline so that all downstream diagnostics (overlap, day concentration, regime concentration, family performance) can begin generating actionable signal for future mutations
expected_benefit: Unblock candidate-to-promotion pipeline so at least some rules survive walk-forward evaluation and populate the watchlist
Enable all downstream diagnostics (overlap, skip quality, family performance, intermarket vs SPY-only) that are currently empty, giving future mutations real signal to work with
Should not compromise robustness severely since 10 instances across 4 days still requires non-trivial recurrence; the maturity tier is research/watchlist-only so no live trading risk
risk: Some promoted candidates may be day-concentrated or regime-concentrated given only 4 days of data, but the maturity gate (research tier) prevents any from reaching live status, and the overlap and concentration diagnostics will flag fragile rules for future pruning
patch.acceptance_hint: Accept if at least 1 candidate rule survives promotion to the watchlist with non-degenerate day spread, enabling downstream diagnostics to produce actionable data for the next mutation cycle

patch.summary: Lower min_support from 30 to 10 to allow candidate rules to survive with only 4 days of data (18949 snapshots)
why: With only 4 trading days and 18949 snapshots, the current min_support=30 requires a rule to fire on 30+ snapshots which is achievable in absolute count but the walk-forward splits across 4 days leave very few snapshots per fold, killing candidates before they can be evaluated

6162 candidates were generated but 0 promoted — the previous experiment tried min_support=15 but was rejected only because it was the first run with no champion baseline, not because the direction was wrong

At 4 days the system needs a lower bar to begin building a watchlist; min_support=10 still requires meaningful recurrence (~10 snapshots across multiple days) while letting the walk-forward evaluator actually score candidates
Once more days accumulate, min_support can be raised back; this is the binding constraint preventing any rule from entering the research watchlist
expected_benefit: Rules should begin surviving into the watchlist/candidate tier so the system can start tracking durability and overlap
Enables all other research knobs (overlap, skip, divergence weights) to have something to act on in subsequent nights
Should not degrade robustness because the evaluator and maturity gating are frozen — no rule can reach live with only 4 days regardless
risk: Lower support admits noisier candidates, but the maturity tier is research-only and walk-forward guardrails still apply; the real risk is minimal because nothing can promote to live at 4 days
patch.acceptance_hint: At least 1 candidate rule survives into the promoted watchlist with support across 2+ distinct days and a positive composite score

patch.summary: Lower min_support from 30 to 10 to allow candidate rules to survive walk-forward with only 4 trading days of data
why: With only 4 trading days and 18949 snapshots, min_support=30 requires a rule to fire on ~30 qualifying snapshots across walk-forward folds, which is extremely hard to achieve when the total data window is tiny — 0 rules were promoted and 6162 candidates were all rejected

The previous experiment tried min_support=15 but was rejected because no champion set existed yet (first run); the underlying logic of lowering support for a small-sample research tier remains correct and now there is a baseline to compare against

At 4 days the system cannot distinguish min_support=30 patterns from noise anyway — lowering to 10 lets the pipeline surface initial watchlist candidates so the engine can begin overlap, concentration, and robustness diagnostics rather than producing empty reports
expected_benefit: Candidate rules should begin surviving walk-forward gating, producing a non-empty watchlist for the first time
Downstream diagnostics (overlap, day concentration, regime concentration, family performance) will finally have data to report, enabling meaningful future mutations
Once more days accumulate, min_support can be tightened back upward — this is a temporary research-tier accommodation
risk: Low support thresholds can admit fragile one-day patterns; mitigated by the existing maturity gating (research tier = watchlist only, no live promotion) and the overlap/concentration guardrails
patch.acceptance_hint: Accept if the challenger run produces at least 1 watchlist candidate rule with non-trivial composite score and the day-concentration diagnostic shows the candidate fires on more than 1 distinct day

patch.summary: Lower min_support from 20 to 15 to allow candidate rules to survive walk-forward with only 4 trading days of data
why: With only 4 trading days and 18949 snapshots, the current min_support=20 is filtering out all 6162 generated candidates — zero rules promoted despite substantial candidate generation
The previous experiment attempted min_support=15 but was rejected only because it was the first run with no champion baseline to compare against, not because it performed poorly

At 4 days, a rule needing 20+ supporting snapshots across walk-forward folds is extremely restrictive; 15 is still conservative (requiring pattern presence across multiple days) while allowing the pipeline to actually promote candidates for evaluation
expected_benefit: Rules should begin passing the support filter, allowing the pipeline to produce a non-empty watchlist and begin meaningful walk-forward evaluation
Overlap, day-concentration, and regime-concentration diagnostics will become populated, enabling targeted future mutations based on real rule quality data
risk: Slightly increased chance of promoting a rule that appeared on only 2-3 of 4 days, but maturity gating at research tier already prevents any live action — this just enables learning
patch.acceptance_hint: Any non-zero number of promoted rules with reasonable composite scores indicates this unblocks the pipeline; watch for day_concentration < 0.5 on promoted rules

decision.patch_summary: first_run
decision.reason: First run produced no rules. No champion set.

patch.summary: Lower min_support to 15 so candidate rules can survive walk-forward gating with only 4 trading days (~18,949 snapshots)
why: With only 4 trading days and 18,949 snapshots, the current min_support of 20 requires a rule to fire in 20+ qualifying snapshots across walk-forward folds — this is extremely hard to achieve when the total data is thin and folds are tiny
6,162 candidates were generated but 0 were promoted, meaning candidates exist but cannot pass the walk-forward support gate; lowering to 15 is the minimum viable threshold to let any rule survive

Two prior attempts to lower min_support were rejected only because there was no champion set to compare against (first-run bootstrap problem), not because the direction was wrong — the system needs at least one promoted rule to establish a champion baseline
15 is the floor of the valid range and is conservative given ~4,700 snapshots per day; a rule firing in 15+ snapshots still represents meaningful recurrence
expected_benefit: Some candidate rules should finally survive walk-forward and get promoted to the watchlist, establishing a champion baseline for future experiments
Once a champion set exists, the acceptance logic can function properly and future mutations can be evaluated on merit rather than rejected as no-baseline
risk: With only 4 days, any rule that passes may be fragile or day-concentrated; however the maturity tier is 'research' (watchlist only) so no live trading exposure exists, and the maturity gating system will prevent premature promotion
patch.acceptance_hint: At least 1 candidate rule promoted to the watchlist, enabling a champion baseline for future experiments; ideally 2+ rules from different families with day_concentration < 0.5

decision.patch_summary: first_run
decision.reason: First run produced no rules. No champion set.

patch.summary: Lower min_composite_score from 0.0 to -2.0 to allow early-stage candidate rules to survive filtering with only 4 days of data
why: 6162 candidates are generated but 0 rules are promoted — the composite scoring gate is killing everything before walk-forward evaluation can assess durability
With only 4 trading days and ~19k snapshots, composite scores will be noisy and conservative; a threshold of 0.0 is too strict for the research tier
Three consecutive experiments tried lowering min_support (which is already at 20, within range) but the bottleneck appears to be composite score filtering, not support count
Lowering min_composite_score to -2.0 lets marginal candidates through to walk-forward where the evaluator's frozen guardrails can properly assess them
expected_benefit: Some of the 6162 candidates should survive to promotion/watchlist, giving the system its first signal about which feature families and rule types are producing durable patterns
Walk-forward evaluation and maturity gating remain frozen, so fragile rules will still be rejected — we just widen the funnel to the evaluator
Future nights will have actual rule diagnostics to guide targeted weight adjustments
risk: More low-quality candidates reach evaluation, increasing compute time slightly, but the frozen evaluator and guardrails prevent any fragile rule from reaching live status
patch.acceptance_hint: At least 1 rule promoted to watchlist or preliminary tier, enabling meaningful diagnostics for subsequent mutations

decision.patch_summary: first_run
decision.reason: First run produced no rules. No champion set.

patch.summary: Widen neighbor_band_pct from 0.10 to 0.20 to allow more candidate rules to find sufficient support across only 4 trading days
why: With only 4 trading days and ~18,949 snapshots, 6,162 candidates are generated but zero survive promotion — the pipeline is too restrictive at the filtering stage

Previous attempts lowered min_support and min_composite_score without success, suggesting the bottleneck is not raw support count or score threshold but rather that candidate rules define overly narrow feature bands that fragment support across near-identical rules

Widening neighbor_band_pct from 0.10 to 0.20 allows each candidate rule's feature thresholds to match a wider range of neighboring snapshots, consolidating support into fewer but more robust candidates that can survive walk-forward evaluation
This is the one remaining major threshold lever that has not been tried in the last 5 experiments
expected_benefit: More candidates accumulate sufficient support to pass walk-forward gating, enabling the first rule promotions
Rules that do survive will represent broader, more durable market states rather than razor-thin threshold artifacts
Overlap filtering downstream will still remove near-duplicates, so rule quality should not degrade
risk: Wider bands may produce rules that are too diffuse to be actionable — but at 0.20 we are still within a reasonable range and the evaluator's robustness checks remain frozen as guardrails
patch.acceptance_hint: At least 1 candidate rule survives to promoted/watchlist status with composite score >= 0, demonstrating the pipeline can produce output with 4 days of data

decision.patch_summary: first_run
decision.reason: First run produced no rules. No champion set.

patch.summary: Lower min_support to 15 snapshots to allow candidate rules to pass filtering given only 4 trading days of data
why: With only 4 trading days and ~18,949 snapshots, 6,162 candidates were generated but 0 rules promoted — the pipeline is generating candidates but none survive the walk-forward/composite gating

Previous experiments lowered min_support and min_composite_score separately but were rejected only because there was no prior champion set to compare against (reason: 'First run produced no rules. No champion set'), not because the mutations caused harm

At 4 days the walk-forward folds are extremely thin; min_support=20 likely causes most rules to fail support checks in at least one fold, killing them despite potentially valid signals across the full sample

min_support=15 is the floor of the allowed range and is the most conservative relaxation that gives the pipeline a chance to produce its first champion set, which is a prerequisite for any future credit assignment
expected_benefit: Some candidate rules should survive walk-forward gating and produce the system's first promoted rulebook, enabling all future A/B comparisons
Rules promoted at support=15 with 18,949 snapshots still require ~15 matching snapshots per fold, which is a meaningful bar — not trivially easy to pass
risk: Rules promoted with lower support may be less durable as more data arrives; however, the maturity tier is 'research' (watchlist only) so no live risk, and the system's maturity gating will prevent premature promotion to live
patch.acceptance_hint: Accept if at least 1 rule is promoted to the watchlist with reasonable composite score and day spread >= 2 out of 4 days

decision.patch_summary: first_run
decision.reason: First run produced no rules. No champion set.

patch.summary: Lower min_composite_score to -5.0 to maximally relax candidate filtering given 6162 candidates generated but 0 promoted with only 4 days of data
why: The pipeline generates 6162 candidates but promotes exactly 0, meaning the composite scoring / filtering gate is too strict for the current 4-day data regime

Previous experiments tried lowering min_support (already at 15-20), lowering min_composite_score to -2.0, and widening neighbor_band_pct to 0.20 — all individually failed, suggesting the composite score gate is still the binding constraint

Lowering min_composite_score to -5.0 (the floor of the valid range) is the most aggressive relaxation available and distinct from the -2.0 already tried — this should let marginal candidates survive into walk-forward evaluation where the maturity gating will still protect against fragile rules reaching live status

With only 4 days and ~19k snapshots, walk-forward folds are extremely short; the composite score likely penalizes rules that have inconsistent fold performance simply due to tiny fold sizes, so relaxing this gate lets more candidates be evaluated holistically
expected_benefit: Should allow at least some candidate rules to survive into the promoted watchlist for the first time, establishing a champion baseline
The maturity tier is research with 4 days — rules cannot reach live status regardless, so relaxing this gate has zero live-trading risk
Establishes a non-empty rule set that future experiments can tighten against
risk: May promote low-quality or noisy rules that are not durable — acceptable because maturity gating prevents any live action and we need a non-empty baseline to begin credit assignment
patch.acceptance_hint: Any non-zero number of promoted rules (entry or skip) appearing in the nightly report, giving the system a champion set baseline to improve from

decision.patch_summary: first_run
decision.reason: First run produced no rules. No champion set.

patch.summary: Lower min_composite_score to -5.0 to let early-stage candidates survive filtering during the 4-day research phase where composite scores are unreliable
why: 6162 candidates were generated but 0 rules promoted across 6 experiments — the pipeline is generating candidates but every single one is being filtered out

Previous experiments already lowered min_support to 15 and widened neighbor_band_pct to 0.20, and lowered min_composite_score to -2.0, but none produced any rules — the composite score floor is still killing all candidates

With only 4 trading days the composite score computation is noisy and penalizing candidates that may be durable — setting the floor to the minimum allowed value (-5.0) maximizes the chance of at least getting candidates into the watchlist for observation

This is the most conservative robustness-preserving move: we are not loosening overlap or support, just allowing the research-tier watchlist to populate so the system can begin learning which candidates survive as more days accumulate
expected_benefit: At least some of the 6162 generated candidates should survive into the watchlist, giving the system observable rules to track and eventually promote
As days accumulate, the composite scores will naturally tighten and weak rules will be pruned — this just prevents premature filtering at 4 days
Does not weaken overlap, support, or any other structural guardrail
risk: Watchlist may temporarily contain low-quality candidates, but maturity gating prevents any from reaching live status
If the real bottleneck is not min_composite_score but some other filtering step, this patch will have no effect — but it eliminates one known binding constraint
patch.acceptance_hint: At least 1 rule appearing in the promoted watchlist or top candidate list, demonstrating the pipeline can pass candidates through filtering with 4 days of data

decision.patch_summary: first_run
decision.reason: First run produced no rules. No champion set.

patch.summary: Widen max_overlap from 0.60 to 0.80 to reduce aggressive deduplication that may be killing all 6162 candidates before any can be promoted
why: 6162 candidates are generated but exactly 0 are promoted — the pipeline is generating signal but the filtering/deduplication stage is eliminating everything

Previous experiments have already tried lowering min_support to 15, lowering min_composite_score to -5.0, and widening neighbor_band_pct to 0.20 — all rejected because no rules were produced, meaning the bottleneck is likely elsewhere

With only 4 days of data, many candidate rules will cover similar snapshots simply because the universe of snapshots is small (~18,949), causing high pairwise Jaccard overlap scores that trigger aggressive deduplication at the 0.60 threshold

Widening max_overlap to 0.80 allows near-duplicate rules to coexist during this early research phase, letting the walk-forward evaluator (not the dedup filter) decide which survive — this is the one major filtering knob that has NOT been tested yet
expected_benefit: At least some of the 6162 candidates should survive deduplication and reach the promotion/watchlist stage, breaking the current zero-rule deadlock
With rules actually promoted, future nightly diagnostics will contain actionable signal about rule quality, family performance, and overlap structure — enabling meaningful subsequent mutations
Should not degrade robustness since walk-forward evaluation and maturity gating are frozen and still enforce quality independently of the overlap filter
risk: If the bottleneck is not overlap-based deduplication but rather walk-forward evaluation killing all candidates, this patch will have no effect — but it is the last untested major threshold knob and eliminates that hypothesis cleanly
patch.acceptance_hint: Any non-zero number of promoted rules (entry or skip) appearing in the nightly output would validate that overlap filtering was the binding constraint

decision.patch_summary: first_run
decision.reason: First run produced no rules. No champion set.

patch.summary: Lower min_support from 20 to 15 snapshots to allow candidate rules to pass walk-forward evaluation with only 4 trading days of data
why: With only 4 trading days and 18949 snapshots, the system generates 6162 candidates but promotes exactly 0 — every single candidate is being filtered out during walk-forward evaluation

Previous experiments tried relaxing min_composite_score to -5.0, widening max_overlap to 0.80, and widening neighbor_band_pct to 0.20, but each was individually rejected because no champion set existed yet — the rejection reason is structural (no baseline), not because the patches were harmful

The min_support=15 patch was also tried individually but rejected for the same structural reason; however, the current hypothesis still shows min_support=20, meaning it was never actually applied — the system needs this as the active baseline to start accumulating any promoted rules at all

At 20 min_support with only 4 days, many candidate rules that fire on 2-3 of 4 days may still not accumulate 20 matching snapshots across walk-forward folds, especially for interaction and divergence rules with tighter conditions
expected_benefit: Some candidates should finally pass walk-forward filtering, establishing the first champion set and unblocking the entire experiment loop
Rules that survive at 15 snapshots across 4 days still represent meaningful recurrence — roughly 0.08% of total snapshots — and as more days accumulate, natural min_support will grow organically
Once any rules promote, future patches can be properly evaluated with credit assignment against a real champion baseline
risk: Lower support threshold could allow some fragile one-day or two-day patterns through, but the maturity gating (research tier, watchlist only) prevents any live action, and the 15-snapshot floor still requires non-trivial recurrence
patch.acceptance_hint: Any nonzero number of promoted rules in the challenger run, establishing a champion baseline for future experiments

decision.patch_summary: first_run
decision.reason: First run produced no rules. No champion set.

patch.summary: Raise neighbor_band_pct from 0.10 to 0.20 to widen the threshold bands around candidate rule levels, increasing the chance that snapshots fall within rule activation zones given sparse 4-day data
why: 6162 candidates are generated but 0 are promoted — previous attempts relaxed min_support, min_composite_score, and max_overlap without success, suggesting the binding constraint is elsewhere

With only 4 trading days and 18949 snapshots, narrow neighbor_band_pct (±10%) means candidate level-based rules require price/feature values to cluster very tightly around a threshold to accumulate support; widening to ±20% lets rules capture broader zones and accumulate enough qualifying snapshots to pass walk-forward evaluation
This is the last major threshold knob that hasn't been tested — all 10 prior experiments focused on min_support, min_composite_score, or max_overlap
expected_benefit: More candidate rules should accumulate sufficient support snapshots to survive walk-forward evaluation, producing the first promoted rules
Broader bands create rules that are more robust to small price/feature variation across days, reducing fragility
Should not degrade rule quality since walk-forward evaluation and composite scoring still gate promotion
risk: Wider bands could produce rules that are too loose/non-specific, capturing noise rather than genuine tradable states — but walk-forward gating should still filter these out
patch.acceptance_hint: At least 1 candidate rule promoted to watchlist; if multiple rules promoted, check that they have reasonable edge and are not pure noise

decision.patch_summary: first_run
decision.reason: First run produced no rules. No champion set.

patch.summary: Increase move_size_preference to 1.5 to bias candidate scoring toward larger tradable moves, since all 6 entry rules have negative net expectancy (best is -$5.14/trade) indicating the system is capturing noisy small moves
why: All 6 promoted entry rules have negative net_exp_usd ranging from -5.14 to -20.94, meaning none are currently profitable after costs — the system is selecting states with insufficient move magnitude

The best rule (D_zdiv_straddle_pct_high_LONG_60m) has 60.2% win rate but still loses money, strongly suggesting wins are too small relative to losses — we need to prefer states with larger expected displacement
Direction balance is 6 LONG / 0 SHORT with regime concentration failure, indicating the system is finding low-conviction directional noise rather than durable larger moves

Mean net expectancy is -$10.35/trade which for 1 /MES ($5/pt) means the average rule loses ~2 points — biasing toward larger move states could flip marginal rules positive by selecting higher-displacement environments
expected_benefit: Rules should capture states where SPY displacement over 30-60m horizons is meaningfully larger, improving the ratio of average win to average loss
Should reduce promotion of rules that fire in low-volatility chop zones where transaction costs dominate
Skip rules should become more effective as the contrast between tradable and non-tradable states sharpens
risk: Higher move_size_preference could reduce support counts by filtering to rarer high-displacement states, potentially dropping below min_support thresholds and reducing the number of promoted rules
patch.acceptance_hint: Accept if mean_net_expectancy_usd improves toward zero or positive (less negative than -10.35) while maintaining at least 4 promoted entry rules with support >= 20

decision.patch_summary: Increase move_size_preference to 1.5 to bias candidate scoring toward larger tradable moves, since all 6 entry rules have negative net expectancy (best is -$5.14/trade) indicating the system is capturing noisy small moves
decision.reason: Challenger failed guardrails: stability. Champion retained.

patch.summary: Raise min_composite_score from 0.0 to 5.0 to filter out the negative-expectancy rules currently being promoted, forcing the system to only promote higher-quality candidates
why: All 6 entry rules have negative net expectancy USD (ranging from -$5.14 to -$20.94), meaning every promoted entry rule loses money after costs — the composite threshold of 0.0 is too permissive and allows junk rules through

The current champion rulebook has 9 promoted rules but none are profitable; raising the quality floor should either force discovery of genuinely better candidates or correctly produce fewer rules rather than promote losing ones
Zero of 12 experiments have been accepted so far, and the last challenger failed stability guardrails — a tighter quality filter is a conservative robustness move rather than an alpha-chasing move
Regime concentration is failing (any_regime_concentration_fail: true), suggesting the promoted rules are fragile single-regime artifacts that a higher composite floor would filter out
expected_benefit: Fewer but higher-quality rules promoted — rules that survive should have better expectancy or at minimum less negative expectancy
Reduced regime concentration failures since low-composite single-regime rules get filtered before promotion
Skip rules (composite 6-24) are unaffected at this threshold since skip evaluation uses separate criteria, preserving skip discipline
risk: With only 6 days of data, raising the floor to 5.0 could result in zero entry rules promoted, yielding a skip-only or empty rulebook — but this is preferable to promoting 6 money-losing rules
patch.acceptance_hint: Accept if the challenger either (a) promotes fewer entry rules but with better mean net expectancy (less negative or positive), or (b) promotes zero entry rules cleanly rather than promoting losers — both outcomes represent genuine improvement over a rulebook where every entry rule loses money

decision.patch_summary: Raise min_composite_score from 0.0 to 5.0 to filter out the negative-expectancy rules currently being promoted, forcing the system to only promote higher-quality candidates
decision.reason: Challenger failed guardrails: composite. Champion retained.

patch.summary: Increase min_support from 20 to 40 to filter out fragile low-sample rules and force the system to discover patterns that recur across more snapshots and days
why: All 6 entry rules have negative net expectancy (best is -$5.14, worst is -$20.94), suggesting the current min_support=20 allows noisy, non-durable patterns to be promoted
Regime concentration is failing (any_regime_concentration_fail=true), indicating rules are overfitting to narrow market conditions rather than capturing robust recurring states
Direction balance is 6 LONG / 0 SHORT, suggesting the system is latching onto regime-specific noise rather than finding genuinely durable signals — raising support should prune the weakest of these

Previous experiments tried raising composite score threshold (failed guardrails: composite) and move_size_preference (failed guardrails: stability) — raising min_support is a different axis that attacks fragility directly at the candidate filtering stage
With 27074 snapshots across 6 days, requiring 40 snapshots is still only 0.15% of the pool — a very modest bar that should still allow valid patterns through while eliminating the thinnest ones
expected_benefit: Promoted rules should have better day spread and reduced regime concentration since they must fire across more snapshots
Net expectancy should improve as the weakest negative-expectancy rules with thin support get pruned before promotion
Walk-forward stability should remain high or improve since higher-support rules are more likely to be stable across folds
risk: Could reduce total promoted entry rules if the system cannot find patterns with 40+ snapshots that also pass walk-forward — but current rules are all negative expectancy so losing some is acceptable
patch.acceptance_hint: Accept if mean net expectancy improves (less negative or positive), regime concentration fail resolves, and at least 3 entry rules are still promoted

decision.patch_summary: Increase min_support from 20 to 40 to filter out fragile low-sample rules and force the system to discover patterns that recur across more snapshots and days
decision.reason: Challenger failed guardrails: composite. Champion retained.

patch.summary: Increase intermarket_weight to 1.5 to boost QQQ divergence signal emphasis, since the divergence family has the best avg composite (44.6) and least negative expectancy (-6.32) among entry families
why: Divergence family rules (both z_score_div based) have the highest avg composite score (44.6) and the least negative avg net expectancy (-$6.32) of all entry rule families, suggesting intermarket/QQQ divergence signals carry the most useful information currently

The system is producing zero SHORT rules and zero sequence rules — boosting intermarket weight may help surface QQQ lead/lag signals that identify SHORT opportunities and diversify the directional balance

All 14 prior experiments have been rejected, and most recent failures were from tightening filters (min_support, min_composite_score) or move_size_preference — those collapsed the candidate pool below guardrails. Boosting a weight that amplifies the best-performing family is a constructive rather than restrictive change

Regime concentration is failing (any_regime_concentration_fail: true), suggesting current rules are too regime-specific. Stronger intermarket emphasis may help discover cross-regime divergence patterns that are more durable
expected_benefit: More divergence and intermarket-derived candidates should be generated and scored higher, increasing the chance of discovering rules with positive or less negative net expectancy
Should not reduce the candidate pool or composite scores since this is an upweight not a filter tightening, avoiding the guardrail failures seen in recent experiments
risk: With only 6 days of data, QQQ divergence signals may be regime-specific and the upweight could amplify a pattern that doesn't generalize beyond this week's market structure
patch.acceptance_hint: Look for: more divergence-family rules promoted, at least one rule with net expectancy > -$5, improved directional balance (at least one SHORT entry), and no worsening of regime concentration

decision.patch_summary: Increase intermarket_weight to 1.5 to boost QQQ divergence signal emphasis, since the divergence family has the best avg composite (44.6) and least negative expectancy (-6.32) among entry families

patch.summary: Increase sequence_family_weights.qqq_lead_confirm to 1.8 to surface QQQ-leading-SPY confirmation sequences, since divergence rules dominate but have negative expectancy and no sequence rules are being promoted despite the family being enabled
why: Zero sequence rules promoted despite the sequence family being enabled — the system is not surfacing any sequence-based patterns, suggesting they are being outcompeted by simpler divergence/level rules that have poor expectancy

All 6 entry rules have negative net expectancy (mean -$7.20/trade), and the best-performing family (divergence) still averages -$1.32/trade — static state detection alone is not capturing durable moves

QQQ lead/confirm sequences should identify moments where QQQ has already moved directionally and SPY is following, which produces higher-conviction entry timing than point-in-time divergence z-scores alone
No prior experiment has tried boosting sequence weights — all 15 experiments focused on thresholds, intermarket_weight, or move_size_preference, so this is a genuinely new search direction
Regime concentration is failing, suggesting current rules are too tied to one market condition — sequence-aware rules that require a preceding QQQ confirmation step should be more regime-robust
expected_benefit: Discovery of at least 1-2 sequence-family rules that combine QQQ lead signals with SPY entry timing, improving net expectancy toward positive territory
Better regime diversity since QQQ-lead-confirm sequences inherently require a multi-bar setup that spans different intraday conditions
Should not degrade skip rule quality or existing divergence rule discovery since we are boosting a currently-zero-output family
risk: With only 6 days of data, sequence patterns requiring multi-bar setups may have even lower support counts, risking fragile rules — but this is bounded by existing min_support=20 guardrail
patch.acceptance_hint: Accept if challenger produces at least one sequence-family rule with positive net expectancy and walk-forward stability >= 0.5, without reducing total promoted rule count below 6

decision.patch_summary: Increase sequence_family_weights.qqq_lead_confirm to 1.8 to surface QQQ-leading-SPY confirmation sequences, since divergence rules dominate but have negative expectancy and no sequence rules are being promoted despite the family being enabled

patch.summary: Increase divergence_family_weights.z_score_div to 1.5 to amplify the z-score divergence sub-family which already produces the highest-composite entry rule (D_zdiv_straddle_pct at 41.4 with perfect WF stability 1.0) and a second strong rule (D_zdiv_awks at 30.1 with 75% win rate and positive expectancy)
why: The two promoted divergence rules both use z-score divergence features (zdiv_straddle_pct_state, zdiv_awks_state) and together form the second-strongest family by avg composite (35.7)
D_zdiv_straddle_pct has perfect walk-forward stability (1.0) with 258 support across 6 days — this is one of the most robust signals in the rulebook
D_zdiv_awks has 75.3% win rate and positive $9.22 net expectancy — the only divergence rule with positive expectancy, suggesting z-score div features carry real edge

Previous experiments tried intermarket_weight (1.5) which failed stability — this is a more targeted boost to the specific divergence sub-family that is already working, rather than broadly boosting all intermarket signals
No sequence rules are being promoted despite being enabled, so the search budget freed by emphasizing proven z-score divergence features should improve overall quality rather than spreading thin
expected_benefit: More candidate rules generated from z-score divergence features, increasing the chance of discovering additional durable divergence states with positive expectancy
The interaction family rule (I_atm_avg_iv SHORT with $34.39 expectancy) should remain unaffected since it uses different feature families
Skip rules should remain stable since they use tape/structure features not divergence features

patch.acceptance_hint: Accept if challenger maintains ≥8 rules above 60% WF stability, mean net expectancy improves above $9.47, and divergence family avg composite stays above 30 without regime concentration failure worsening

The best rule by expectancy (I_atm_avg_iv interaction SHORT at $34.39) captures a meaningfully larger move than the average, suggesting the search should systematically prefer larger-move candidates

Previous failed experiments all targeted weight/threshold adjustments that hit composite or stability guardrails; move_size_preference is an orthogonal knob that hasn't been tested yet and should reshape the candidate ranking rather than the feature pipeline
With only 7 days and regime concentration flagged as failing, preferring larger moves should help filter out low-conviction micro-noise patterns that are regime-fragile
expected_benefit: Higher average net expectancy per promoted rule by down-ranking rules that capture sub-$10 moves
Better stop/target usefulness for 1 /MES since larger expected moves create more room for realistic stop placement
Should not harm rule count or stability since we are re-ranking candidates, not eliminating feature families

decision.patch_summary: Increase move_size_preference to 1.4 to favor rules capturing larger tradable moves, addressing the weak mean net expectancy ($9.47) and negative-expectancy rules currently promoted

why: The top-composite entry rule L_otm_put_pct_low_LONG_60m has only 36 support snapshots across 5 days with a terrible net expectancy of -$20.38, yet it ranks #1 by composite (59.04) — this is a fragile low-sample artifact inflating the rulebook

The regime_concentration_fail is true, suggesting some rules are regime-specific; raising min_support from 20 to 30 (moderate increase, not the 40 that previously failed) should prune the most fragile rules without collapsing the entire candidate pool
The prior attempt at min_support=40 failed the composite guardrail, suggesting 40 was too aggressive; 30 is a more conservative step that still filters the worst offenders like the 36-support rule
The strong rules (L_nope_Q1 at 3036 support, I_atm_avg_iv at 1718 support, D_zdiv_awks at 308 support) will all easily survive this threshold increase, so the quality core is preserved
expected_benefit: Removes fragile low-support rules with negative expectancy that currently rank high on composite, improving mean net expectancy across promoted rules
Should reduce regime concentration by eliminating rules that only fire in narrow windows
Mean walk-forward stability should improve as low-support rules tend to have unstable WF performance
risk: May reduce total rule count slightly, but the 40-threshold previously failed composite guardrail so 30 is a gentler step that should retain enough candidates

patch.summary: Increase feature_family_weights.structure to 1.5 to amplify structural features, since the single interaction rule using structural_gate_q5 has by far the best net expectancy ($34.39) and perfect WF stability (1.0), suggesting structure features are underweighted relative to their signal quality
why: The interaction rule I_atm_avg_iv_q5_structural_gate_q5 SHORT_60m has the best net expectancy at $34.39 (3.6x the mean) with perfect 1.0 WF stability, and it depends on structural_gate_q5 as a core condition
The skip rule SKIP_weak_gate_30m also uses structural_gate and has 7-day coverage — structure features appear to be high-signal for both entry and skip quality

No sequence rules are being promoted despite being enabled, and level rules dominate count (3) but include the worst rule (L_otm_put_pct at -$20.38) — boosting structure should help the interaction/divergence families that use structural features compete better in candidate ranking

All feature_family_weights are currently at 1.0 (default), so this is an unexplored direction; the regime_concentration_fail flag suggests we need more robust structural context to avoid regime-specific rules
expected_benefit: More interaction and divergence candidates that incorporate structural context should surface, improving average net expectancy
Better skip discrimination via stronger structural_gate weighting should reduce regime concentration failures
WF stability should remain high since the structural features already show stability in promoted rules
risk: Boosting structure may over-concentrate candidates around structural_gate variants, potentially increasing overlap — but current max_overlap at 0.6 should guard against this

decision.patch_summary: Increase feature_family_weights.structure to 1.5 to amplify structural features, since the single interaction rule using structural_gate_q5 has by far the best net expectancy ($34.39) and perfect WF stability (1.0), suggesting structure features are underweighted relative to their signal quality

patch.summary: Tighten max_overlap from 0.6 to 0.45 to reduce near-duplicate rules and force the system to promote more diverse, less correlated states
why: The rulebook has 6 entry rules but 4 are LONG and 3 are in the level family — this clustering suggests overlapping conditions are passing through the current 0.6 Jaccard threshold

The worst rule (L_otm_put_pct_low, support=36, net_exp=-$20.38) likely overlaps significantly with L_nope_Q1 (both level-family LONG 60m rules using options flow quintiles), and tighter overlap filtering would force one to yield to the other
The regime_concentration_fail=true flag indicates the rulebook is not diverse enough across market conditions — reducing overlap should push the search toward rules that activate in different regimes

Recent experiments raising weights (structure 1.5, z_score_div 1.5) both failed composite guardrails, suggesting the issue is not underweighting a family but rather crowding within families — an overlap tightening addresses this structurally
expected_benefit: Fewer near-duplicate rules promoted, forcing the system to find rules that cover distinct market states
Negative-expectancy rules that survive only because they slightly differ from a stronger overlapping rule should be filtered out
Better regime diversity as the system must fill slots with rules from different conditions rather than variations of the same signal
Mean net expectancy should improve as the weakest overlapping rules are pruned
risk: Could reduce total rule count if not enough diverse candidates exist, potentially lowering total composite — but 6297 candidates suggests ample diversity in the pool

decision.patch_summary: Tighten max_overlap from 0.6 to 0.45 to reduce near-duplicate rules and force the system to promote more diverse, less correlated states

patch.summary: Increase intermarket_weight from 1.0 to 1.4 to boost QQQ divergence signal emphasis, since divergence family rules show high composite scores and perfect WF stability but only 2 promoted rules versus 3 level rules, suggesting intermarket signals are underweighted relative to their quality
why: The divergence family has the highest avg_composite (39.7) among entry families and includes D_zdiv_straddle_pct with perfect 1.0 WF stability and 258 support across 6 days, yet only 2 divergence rules were promoted versus 3 level rules

The single interaction rule using structural_gate has $34.39 net expectancy with 1.0 WF stability, showing intermarket/structural signals are high quality; boosting intermarket_weight should help discover more of these durable cross-asset states

Regime concentration is flagged as failing (any_regime_concentration_fail: true), and intermarket divergence signals are inherently more regime-robust than single-asset level rules since they capture relative dynamics

Previous attempts to boost structure weights (1.5) and z_score_div weights (1.5) both failed composite guardrails, suggesting those jumps were too large; a moderate 1.0→1.4 intermarket_weight increase is a gentler way to achieve similar directional emphasis across all intermarket features
expected_benefit: More divergence and intermarket-aware rules promoted, improving regime robustness and reducing regime concentration failures
Higher average net expectancy as intermarket rules like the zdiv and interaction families show better dollar performance than pure level rules
Skip rules should not degrade since they are based on time-of-day and efficiency features, not intermarket signals
risk: With only 7 days of data, boosting intermarket emphasis could overfit to the specific SPY/QQQ relationship observed in this short window; if the divergence patterns are regime-specific rather than durable, this could hurt out-of-sample
patch.acceptance_hint: Composite improvement ≥5% with no degradation in WF stability, ideally promoting ≥3 divergence/interaction rules with positive net expectancy and reducing regime concentration failure

main_concerns: 4 out of 5 recent experiments have been rejected for failing composite guardrails, suggesting the system is at or near a local optimum and weight/scalar adjustments are systematically failing — this patch follows the same pattern with a different knob
Only 7 days of data with regime concentration already failing — boosting intermarket weight amplifies regime-specific SPY/QQQ correlation patterns that may be artifacts of this particular week

The reasoning conflates divergence family performance with intermarket_weight, but intermarket_weight affects all intermarket features globally, not just the z-score divergence sub-family that's performing well — this is a blunt instrument for a targeted observation

The proposer acknowledges that boosting structure weights (1.5) and z_score_div weights (1.5) both failed, then argues 1.0→1.4 on a different knob is 'gentler' — but this is a different axis entirely, not a smaller version of those failed experiments
what_is_good: The divergence family does show genuinely strong diagnostics: highest avg_composite (39.7), good WF stability on the zdiv_straddle rule
The patch is a single scalar change, which is appropriately bounded
The proposer correctly identifies regime concentration as a real problem worth addressing
The risk section honestly acknowledges the 7-day data limitation
failure_modes: Boosting intermarket_weight could surface more QQQ-correlated rules that all fire in the same regime, worsening regime concentration rather than fixing it
With 7 days the SPY/QQQ relationship is dominated by whatever macro regime this week exhibited — amplifying those signals is essentially fitting to one regime
Could suppress or crowd out the strong interaction rule (I_atm_avg_iv with $34.39 net exp) if new intermarket-weighted rules overlap with it
Likely to fail composite guardrails like the 4 previous experiments, wasting a nightly experiment slot
critic_verdict.maturity_check: PRELIMINARY tier with 7 days is insufficient to validate intermarket relationship stability. The divergence rules that look good (zdiv_straddle 6 days, zdiv_awks 6 days) span nearly the entire dataset — there is no holdout regime to confirm these patterns persist. Boosting emphasis on intermarket signals at this maturity is premature.
critic_verdict.boundedness_check: Yes, this is truly one scalar change (intermarket_weight 1.0→1.4). Bounded and testable. However, it's a global multiplier that affects all intermarket features, making the blast radius larger than it appears.
kill_conditions: Composite degrades by any amount (consistent with 4/5 recent failures)
Regime concentration failure persists or worsens
Number of promoted rules with negative net expectancy increases
WF stability mean drops below 0.80
Day concentration rises above 0.40

patch.summary: Increase intermarket_weight from 1.0 to 1.4 to amplify QQQ divergence signals, since the two divergence-family rules (zdiv_straddle_pct and zdiv_awks) both show strong walk-forward stability and the system has regime concentration failure suggesting it needs more cross-asset confirmation
why: Divergence family has the highest avg_composite (39.7) among entry families and both promoted divergence rules use z-score divergence features that inherently rely on SPY/QQQ intermarket comparison, yet intermarket_weight is at default 1.0

The system reports any_regime_concentration_fail=true, indicating rules are clustering in specific regimes; stronger intermarket weighting should help discover rules that are confirmed across SPY/QQQ behavior rather than being regime-specific

The best single rule by net expectancy ($34.39) is an interaction rule combining IV and structural_gate, while divergence rules have modest expectancy ($3-9); boosting intermarket weight should help divergence rules capture larger cross-asset dislocations with better dollar expectancy

Previous failed experiments tried amplifying individual feature families (structure 1.5) and divergence sub-families (z_score_div 1.5) but failed composite guardrails - a moderate intermarket_weight increase (1.4) is a gentler lever that influences ranking/scoring across all intermarket-related features simultaneously rather than just one family
expected_benefit: Divergence and QQQ-related rules should rank higher in candidate generation, potentially promoting rules with better cross-asset confirmation and larger net expectancy
Regime concentration should improve as intermarket-confirmed rules are inherently less regime-dependent
Should not harm the strong interaction rule (atm_avg_iv + structural_gate SHORT) since it does not compete directly with intermarket features
risk: With only 7 days of data, QQQ/SPY relationship patterns may not yet be stable enough to reward heavier intermarket emphasis; could bias toward a particular short-term SPY/QQQ correlation regime
patch.acceptance_hint: Look for: more divergence/sequence rules promoted, improved regime concentration (no fail), mean net expectancy above $10, and composite improvement without losing the strong interaction SHORT rule

main_concerns: Only 7 trading days of data — far too little evidence to conclude that SPY/QQQ intermarket relationships are stable enough to warrant a 40% weight increase
The causal chain is speculative: the two promoted divergence rules use z-score features, but the claim that intermarket_weight=1.4 will improve regime concentration is narrative, not evidence-driven

4 of the last 5 experiments failed composite guardrails; the one accepted change (move_size_preference 1.4) was a different lever — there is no diagnostic evidence that intermarket_weight specifically is the binding constraint

The regime_concentration_fail flag is cited as justification, but increasing intermarket weight does not mechanistically fix regime concentration — it just reweights feature importance in search, which could easily cluster rules into a different single regime (the one where QQQ leads SPY)
what_is_good: The patch is a single scalar change, which is bounded and testable
The rationale correctly notes that prior per-family weight boosts failed, and this is a broader lever
The risk acknowledgment about 7 days and short-term correlation regime is honest and appropriate
failure_modes: At 7 days, the SPY/QQQ divergence patterns may be dominated by 1-2 days of correlated or decorrelated behavior — amplifying intermarket weight could lock in a regime artifact
Could suppress non-intermarket rules that are currently strong (e.g., the interaction SHORT rule with $34.39 expectancy) if the ranking reweighting pulls attention toward QQQ-derived features
Could create a different regime concentration failure (QQQ-leading regime) rather than fixing the existing one

Prior experiments at similar magnitudes (1.5x for z_score_div, 1.5x for structure) all failed composite guardrails — a 1.4x intermarket boost is in the same range and likely faces the same fate, wasting an experiment cycle
critic_verdict.maturity_check: FAIL — 7 days (preliminary tier) is insufficient to characterize intermarket relationships. QQQ/SPY correlation regime can shift week-to-week. Amplifying intermarket weight at this maturity level is premature and risks locking in a transient pattern.
critic_verdict.boundedness_check: PASS — single scalar change from 1.0 to 1.4, clearly bounded and reversible.
kill_conditions: If run despite rejection: reject if composite drops by any amount
Reject if regime_concentration_fail remains true or worsens
Reject if the strong interaction SHORT rule (I_atm_avg_iv + structural_gate, $34.39) is lost from promoted rules
Reject if number of divergence-only rules increases without net expectancy improvement
Reject if mean WF stability drops below 0.80

patch.summary: Increase intermarket_weight from 1.0 to 1.4 to amplify QQQ/intermarket signal contribution, since divergence rules show strong WF stability (D_zdiv_straddle 1.0, D_zdiv_awks 0.667) and the regime_concentration_fail suggests we need more cross-asset confirmation to improve robustness
why: Divergence family rules (z_score_div based) show high WF stability (1.0 and 0.667) and decent composite scores (45.6 and 33.9), indicating intermarket SPY/QQQ signals carry durable information that is currently underweighted

any_regime_concentration_fail is true, meaning the current rulebook is too concentrated in certain regimes — increasing intermarket weight should force more diverse cross-asset confirmation and reduce regime dependency

The best single rule by net expectancy (I_atm_avg_iv_q5_structural_gate SHORT, $34.39) uses structural features but the divergence family has 0 SHORT rules — boosting intermarket weight may help discover SHORT-side divergence rules that improve direction balance

Previous failed experiments targeted individual feature weights (structure 1.5) and divergence sub-family weights (z_score_div 1.5) which were too aggressive; intermarket_weight is a gentler global knob that lifts all QQQ-related signals proportionally
expected_benefit: More divergence and intermarket rules should get promoted, improving family diversity beyond level-dominated rulebook
Regime concentration should decrease as QQQ cross-confirmation adds an independent validation axis

main_concerns: Only 7 trading days of data — any intermarket relationship observed is statistically unreliable and highly susceptible to regime-specific correlation patterns in this tiny window

4 out of 5 recent experiments have been rejected for failing composite guardrails, and this patch follows the same pattern of boosting a weight by 40% based on thin evidence — the system is telling us the current champion is hard to beat

The causal logic is weak: 'regime_concentration_fail is true therefore increase intermarket_weight' is a narrative jump — regime concentration could be caused by the 7-day sample being dominated by one regime, not by insufficient QQQ signal weight

The claim that boosting intermarket_weight will help discover SHORT-side divergence rules is speculative — there's no diagnostic evidence that SHORT divergence candidates exist but are being ranked below threshold

failure_modes: With only 7 days, SPY-QQQ correlation structure is one-regime noise — a 40% intermarket boost could overfit to whatever correlation pattern dominated this week
Could demote currently good non-intermarket rules (like the I_atm_avg_iv interaction rule with $34.39 expectancy) by shifting ranking weight toward QQQ-dependent candidates
If this week happened to be a divergence-rich week between SPY and QQQ, the boost will amplify a transient pattern
Follows the exact same pattern as the z_score_div 1.5 weight boost that already failed — this is a softer version of the same thesis that was rejected
critic_verdict.maturity_check: PRELIMINARY tier with 7 days is far too thin to make inferences about intermarket signal reliability. The divergence rules have only 5-6 day coverage each. Increasing intermarket weight by 40% based on 2 divergence rules seen across 5-6 days is not justified by the data maturity.

kill_conditions: If run anyway: reject if composite drops at all
Reject if regime_concentration_fail remains true (since that was the stated motivation)
Reject if divergence family count does not increase
Reject if mean WF stability drops below 0.80

patch.summary: Increase intermarket_weight from 1.0 to 1.4 to boost QQQ-related feature and divergence signal emphasis, since the system has 127 divergence columns and strong QQQ-interaction rules but zero divergence or sequence family rules promoted — only interaction rules survive, suggesting intermarket signals need more weight to compete

Regime concentration is flagged as failing (any_regime_concentration_fail: true), and boosting intermarket weight should help discover rules that are robust across regimes by leveraging SPY/QQQ relative behavior which varies across regimes

Better regime diversification as intermarket divergence signals naturally capture regime transitions
Should not degrade the strong existing SHORT interaction rules since they already incorporate IV features that correlate with intermarket dynamics

main_concerns: With only 8 trading days and preliminary maturity tier, boosting intermarket_weight by 40% is too aggressive — the system has insufficient data to distinguish genuine intermarket signal from coincidental regime correlation

Regime concentration failure is flagged but with only 8 days there may be only 1-2 regimes present — boosting intermarket weight to 'fix' regime concentration with so little data is chasing a metric that isn't yet meaningful

kill_conditions: Mean composite score drops by more than 5%
Net expectancy of newly promoted divergence/sequence rules is negative
Regime concentration worsens or stays flagged true
Rule count increases but average composite decreases (rule explosion)
Any newly promoted rule has fewer than 5 unique days coverage

patch.summary: Increase intermarket_weight from 1.0 to 1.4 to boost QQQ-informed feature and rule scoring, since the top LONG rule already uses qqq_atm_avg_iv and zero divergence/sequence rules are being promoted despite those families being enabled
why: All 6 promoted entry rules are interaction-family only; divergence and sequence families produced zero promoted rules despite being enabled, suggesting intermarket signals need more weight to compete in composite scoring

The third-ranked entry rule (I_tick_q5_Q0_qqq_atm_avg_iv_gt_LONG_60m) explicitly uses QQQ IV as a condition with perfect WF stability 1.0, indicating QQQ-derived features carry real signal that could be amplified

Regime concentration is flagging a fail (any_regime_concentration_fail: true), and stronger intermarket weighting should help diversify rule discovery across regimes since SPY/QQQ divergence states vary across regimes

The 127 divergence columns (33-39% of feature pool across tiers) are producing zero promoted rules at current weighting, representing significant untapped search surface that intermarket emphasis could unlock
expected_benefit: More divergence and sequence rules should clear composite thresholds and get promoted, improving family diversity beyond pure interaction rules
Better regime diversification as QQQ lead/lag and divergence signals tend to be regime-varying, potentially fixing the regime concentration fail
Should not degrade the strong existing SHORT interaction rules since they are IV-based and not directly competing with intermarket scoring
risk: With only 8 days of data, QQQ intermarket signal could be regime-specific; however 1.4 is a moderate increase that keeps SPY-native features dominant
patch.acceptance_hint: Look for at least one divergence or sequence rule promoted alongside maintained or improved composite score and no new regime concentration failure

decision.patch_summary: Increase intermarket_weight from 1.0 to 1.4 to boost QQQ-informed feature and rule scoring, since the top LONG rule already uses qqq_atm_avg_iv and zero divergence/sequence rules are being promoted despite those families being enabled
decision.reason: Challenger improves composite by 3.1% with net expectancy delta $+2.91, stability delta +0.0000. All guardrails passed.

patch.summary: Increase divergence_family_weights.z_score_div to 1.8 to promote more z-score divergence rules, since the single divergence rule (D_zdiv_gex_normalized) already shows strong walk-forward stability (1.0) and positive expectancy ($22.17) but divergence family is severely underrepresented (1 rule vs 5 interaction)
why: Only 1 divergence rule was promoted vs 5 interaction rules, despite divergence having competitive avg_composite (37.21) and positive net expectancy ($22.17) with perfect WF stability (1.0)

The single promoted divergence rule uses zdiv_gex_normalized_state, indicating z_score_div sub-family has signal but is being outcompeted at default weight; boosting it should surface more diverse divergence candidates

Regime concentration is flagged as failing (any_regime_concentration_fail: true), suggesting the interaction-heavy rulebook is too concentrated; adding divergence rules should improve regime diversity
Direction balance is heavily SHORT-skewed (5 SHORT vs 1 LONG); divergence signals often capture mean-reversion states that could surface LONG rules and improve balance
expected_benefit: More divergence-family rules promoted, improving family diversity beyond interaction-dominated rulebook
Better regime robustness by surfacing states that rely on SPY/QQQ z-score divergence rather than level-based IV quintile interactions
Should not degrade existing interaction rules since this only boosts relative scoring of z_score_div candidates
risk: z_score_div features may produce rules with lower support or more day-concentrated patterns; however the existing zdiv rule has 2397 support across 7 days, suggesting the family is well-distributed
patch.acceptance_hint: Accept if divergence family rule count increases to 2+ while maintaining mean WF stability >= 0.85 and mean net expectancy >= $30, without regime concentration worsening

decision.patch_summary: Increase divergence_family_weights.z_score_div to 1.8 to promote more z-score divergence rules, since the single divergence rule (D_zdiv_gex_normalized) already shows strong walk-forward stability (1.0) and positive expectancy ($22.17) but divergence family is severely underrepresented (1 rule vs 5 interaction)
decision.reason: Challenger failed guardrails: composite. Champion retained.
